text.title: Growth SEO Fundamentals Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Growth SEO Fundamentals application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Fix Technical SEO blockers (crawlability, indexability, Core Web Vitals, HTTPS, canonicals) BEFORE investing in On-Page or Off-Page work. The pillar order is Technical → On-Page → Off-Page; reversing it spends content + link budget on a site that cannot rank.
rule.rationale: A single critical Technical issue (robots.txt blocking, no sitemap, broken canonicals) creates a ranking ceiling no amount of content or backlinks can break.

rule.statement: Classify the target query's intent (Informational / Commercial / Transactional / Navigational) BEFORE writing the page; produce the content format that matches the intent. A how-to guide ranking for a comparison query under-performs regardless of keyword density.
rule.rationale: Google ranks for user satisfaction, not keyword matching. Format mismatch causes high bounce, which signals low quality and demotes the page.

rule.statement: Every indexable page MUST satisfy the on-page baseline: title ≤60 chars with primary keyword, meta description 120-160 chars, single H1 containing the keyword, URL slug short + hyphenated, ≥1 descriptive internal link, ≥1 authoritative external citation, schema markup appropriate to the page type.
rule.rationale: These elements collectively signal what the page is about and demonstrate authority + relevance; missing any one of them removes a ranking signal.

rule.statement: Acquire links by quality, not volume: one editorial link from a DR ≥60 relevant site outweighs 50 directory / forum links. Refuse purchased links, link farms, and excessive reciprocal-linking schemes; they trigger algorithmic + manual penalties.
rule.rationale: Google's link evaluator considers authority, relevance, anchor naturalness, and placement; low-quality patterns risk Penguin-style demotion or manual action.

rule.statement: Track these five metrics monthly: organic traffic month-over-month, keywords ranking in top 10, domain authority on a 6-12-month trend, Core Web Vitals (all green in CrUX), organic CTR (≥3%). Stagnation in any one signals a pillar-level bottleneck to investigate before adding work.
rule.rationale: These five metrics span all three pillars; reporting on one in isolation hides which pillar is failing and routes effort to the wrong fix.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
